2026-04-16T02:16:37.7537084Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-16T02:16:37.7537910Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-16T02:16:37.7538330Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-16T02:16:37.7538669Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-16T02:16:37.7538953Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-16T02:16:37.7539169Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-16T02:16:37.7539312Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-16T02:16:37.7539477Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-16T02:16:37.7539629Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-16T02:16:37.7539837Z [36;1m{[0m
2026-04-16T02:16:37.7540019Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_api_key=${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-16T02:16:37.7540280Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-16T02:16:37.7540518Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"details":{"description":"Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"},"name":"report_incomplete"},{"description":"Post a routing comment @mentioning team owners on the triggering issue; bypasses safe-outputs mention neutralization","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"message":{"description":"The comment body text without any @mentions or @ symbols","type":"string"},"owners":{"description":"Comma-separated GitHub usernames to notify, without the @ prefix (e.g. 'user1, user2, Azure/team-name')","type":"string"}},"required":["message","owners"],"type":"object"},"name":"mention_owners"}]}}}

2026-04-16T02:19:19.4050208Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-16T02:17:44.146590499Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58199,\"title\":\"[Communication Messages] Docs: README missing Conversation APIs, new message types, and Entra ID auth example\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.Communication.Messages`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/communication/Azure.Communication.Messages/`\\n**Triggered by:** commit [REDACTED] (PR #56911) by `@MaiLinhP`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #56911 migrated `Azure.Communication.Messages` to the new TypeSpec emitter. As part of the broader evolution of the package (1.2.0-beta.1 through 1.3.0-beta.3), the library now ships three public clients:\\n\\n- **`NotificationMessagesClient`** – sends WhatsApp notification messages\\n- **`MessageTemplateClient`** – retrieves WhatsApp message templates\\n- **`ConversationAdministrationClient`** (added 1.3.0-beta.1, Unified Messaging) – creates/lists/manages conversation threads\\n- **`ConversationThreadClient`** (added 1.3.0-beta.1, Unified Messaging) – sends and receives messages within a conversation thread\\n\\nNew message content types were also added: `InteractiveNotificationContent`, `ReactionNotificationContent`, `StickerNotificationContent` (1.2.0-beta.1), and read receipt / typing indicator support (1.3.0-beta.2).\\n\\nThe CHANGELOG entry for `1.3.0-beta.3 (Unreleased)` is empty despite the generator migration occurring in this version.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- `ConversationAdministrationClient` and `ConversationThreadClient` are not mentioned anywhere in README.md — Key concepts, Examples, or Next steps\\n- `ConversationThreadClientSettings` is not documented\\n- Interactive messages (`InteractiveNotificationContent`), reaction messages (`ReactionNotificationContent`), and sticker messages (`StickerNotificationContent`) are absent from the Examples section\\n- Read receipt and typing indicator functionality (1.3.0-beta.2) has no usage example\\n- The README recommends Microsoft Entra ID in production but provides no code sample for Entra ID authentication for any client\\n- The \\u0026#34;Key concepts\\u0026#34; section is a single line covering only `NotificationMessagesClient`; all other clients are unlisted\\n- CHANGELOG `1.3.0-beta.3 (Unreleased)` is empty — generator migration (and any related internal changes) should be noted under **Other Changes**\\n- No test-backed snippets (`#region Snippet:` markers) exist for any README code example; all samples are raw inline code blocks\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n#### 1a. Update README.md\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/communication/Azure.Communication.Messages/README.md`\\n**Action:** Edit — replace / expand the following sections.\\n\\n**Key concepts** — replace the current single-line section with:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Key concepts\\n\\n- **`NotificationMessagesClient`** – Sends outbound notification messages (text, media, template) over WhatsApp channels.\\n- **`MessageTemplateClient`** – Lists WhatsApp message templates registered with your Communication Services resource.\\n- **`ConversationAdministrationClient`** – Creates and manages Unified Messaging conversation threads.\\n- **`ConversationThreadClient`** – Sends and receives messages within an existing conversation thread, including interactive messages, reactions, stickers, read receipts, and typing indicators.\\n```\\n\\n**Authenticate the client** — add an Entra ID snippet placeholder after the connection-string snippet:\\n\\n```markdown\\n#### Using Microsoft Entra ID (recommended for production)\\n\\n```\\nvar endpoint = new Uri(\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;);\\nvar credential = new DefaultAzureCredential();\\nNotificationMessagesClient notificationMessagesClient = new NotificationMessagesClient(endpoint, credential);\\nConversationAdministrationClient conversationAdminClient = new ConversationAdministrationClient(endpoint, credential);\\n```\\n```\\n\\n**Examples** — add subsections for the undocumented clients and message types:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Work with Conversation threads (Unified Messaging)\\n\\n#### Create a conversation thread\\n\\n```\\nstring channelId = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\nstring topic = \\u0026#34;Support conversation\\u0026#34;;\\nCreateConversationOptions options = new CreateConversationOptions(channelId)\\n{\\n    Topic = topic\\n};\\nCreateConversationResult result = await conversationAdminClient.CreateConversationAsync(options);\\nConsole.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Conversation thread id: {result.ConversationId}\\u0026#34;);\\n```\\n\\n#### Send a message in a conversation thread\\n\\n```\\nstring conversationId = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\nConversationThreadClient threadClient = conversationAdminClient.GetConversationThreadClient(conversationId);\\nvar textContent = new TextConversationMessageContent(\\u0026#34;Hello from the SDK!\\u0026#34;);\\nSendConversationMessageResult result = await threadClient.SendMessageAsync(textContent);\\nConsole.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Message id: {result.MessageId}\\u0026#34;);\\n```\\n\\n#### List conversation threads\\n\\n```\\nAsyncPageable conversations = conversationAdminClient.GetConversationsAsync();\\nawait foreach (ConversationItem conversation in conversations)\\n{\\n    Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Conversation: {conversation.ConversationId}\\u0026#34;);\\n}\\n```\\n\\n### Send interactive and reaction messages (WhatsApp)\\n\\n#### Send an interactive message\\n\\n```\\nvar recipientList = new List { \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34; };\\nvar interactiveContent = new InteractiveNotificationContent(channelRegistrationId, recipientList, interactiveMessage);\\nSendMessageResult result = await notificationMessagesClient.SendAsync(interactiveContent);\\nConsole.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Message id: {result.Receipts[0].MessageId}\\u0026#34;);\\n```\\n\\n#### Send a reaction message\\n\\n```\\nvar recipientList = new List { \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34; };\\nvar reactionContent = new ReactionNotificationContent(channelRegistrationId, recipientList, \\u0026#34;\\\\uD83D\\\\uDC4D\\u0026#34;, \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;);\\nSendMessageResult result = await notificationMessagesClient.SendAsync(reactionContent);\\nConsole.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Message id: {result.Receipts[0].MessageId}\\u0026#34;);\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code the test framework in use) containing individual `[Test]` methods, one per snippet:\\n\\n```csharp\\n// sdk/communication/Azure.Communication.Messages/tests/Samples/Sample_MessagesConversation.cs\\n\\nusing System;\\nusing System.Collections.Generic;\\nusing System.Threading.Tasks;\\nusing ConversationAdministrationClient(endpoint, credential);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Used only for README snippet extraction\\u0026#34;)]\\n CreateConversationOptions(channelId)\\n            {\\n                Topic = topic\\n            };\\n            CreateConversationResult result = await conversationAdminClient.CreateConversationAsync(options);\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Conversation thread id: {result.ConversationId}\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n ConversationAdministrationClient conversationAdminClient = null!;\\n            #region Snippet:MessagesListConversations\\n            AsyncPageable conversations = conversationAdminClient.GetConversationsAsync();\\n            await foreach (ConversationItem conversation in conversations)\\n            {\\n                Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Conversation: {conversation.ConversationId}\\u0026#34;);\\n            }\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\n\\u0026gt; **Note:** Verify the exact type names (`CreateConversationOptions`, `CreateConversationResult`, `ConversationItem`, etc.) against the current API surface in `sdk/communication/Azure.Communication.Messages/api/Azure.Communication.Messages.net10.0.cs` before writing the test file.
